source.selection_hint: select a test dialog where prior failure should reduce confidence and alter subsequent response style
source.target_mechanism: plastic writeback after failure and later decision change
source.expected_observable: downstream decision change on a paired follow-up after explicit or implicit negative feedback

raw_source_record.input_prompt:
You are Sheldon, a long-term conversation agent capable of interacting with multiple users. 
Based on the [Dialog History] provided, please answer the given [Question]. 
Note the following points: 
1. Your responses should solely rely on the retrieved dialog history. If the information in the dialog history is insufficient to answer the question, you must admit that you don't know the answer.
2. This question is being asked in the context of May 4, 2010.

[Question] In light of the intellectually stimulating exchange from January 12, 2010, what collectible did Raj leverage as a persuasive incentive to secure Sheldon's presence at a collegiate social gathering?
[Answer]
raw_source_record.dialog_bm25_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [January 20, 2009, Session #227]:
Speaker Pennysays : Hello Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hi.
Speaker Pennysays : What is this?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a questionnaire I devised. I’m having some difficulty bonding with a colleague at work, so I’m doing a little research to better understand why my current friends like me.
Speaker Pennysays : Yes. Well, that is a good question. But is this really the best way to figure it out?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, I agree, the social sciences are largely hokum. But, short of putting electrodes in your brain and monitoring your response to my companionship, this is the best I can do.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, question 1. Rank the following aspects of Sheldon Cooper in order of appeal. Intelligence. Ruthless attention to hygiene. Playfulness. Java applet writing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know, I may have started off with a fairly obvious one. You did an aspect of my most appealing trait, playfulness. Why don’t you just go ahead and write that number 1. I’m afraid you’re on your own for the rest. It should take you no more than 3 hours.
Speaker Pennysays : Wait! How many questions are on this thing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Only 211. Don’t worry, in deference to you, I’ve kept them on a high school graduate reading level.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks pal.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You got it, buddy.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon honey, did you ever consider making friends by being, I don’t know, pleasant?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well that’s certainly a thought-provoking hypothesis. May I suggest it as the topic for your essay.

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [September 23, 2010, Session #471]:
Speaker Pennysays : Does NASA know you’re using that thing as a napkin holder?
Speaker Howardsays : You kidding? They still think it’s in a secure locker at JPL.
Speaker Pennysays : You stole it?
Speaker Howardsays : Borrowed. The trick is to carry it out to your car like you own it.
Speaker alert)says : Excuse me. Oh. Amy’s at the dry cleaners, and she’s made a very amusing pun. “I don’t care for perchloroethylene, and I don’t like glycol ether.” Get it? She doesn’t like glycol ether. Sounds like either.  L-O-L.
Speaker Pennysays : Who’s Amy?
Speaker Leonardsays : His girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon has a girlfriend?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : How long has this been going on?
Speaker Leonardsays : Four months.
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Are you telling me, for the past four months I have been asking you what’s new and you never thought to go with Sheldon has a girlfriend?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Ah, du-du-du-du-du. How did they meet?
Speaker Howardsays : Raj and I entered Sheldon’s information on a dating site, and it spit out Amy Farrah Fowler.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, my God! Sheldon and Amy.
Speaker Howardsays : Or, as we call them, Shamy.
Speaker (squeals)says : Shamy. I am so digging the Shamy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, everyone pay attention. Yes, I have a friend named Amy Farrah Fowler. Yes, she is female. Yes, we communicate on a daily basis, but no, she is not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, well, what do you communicate about?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, my work in physics, her work in neurobiology, and most recently, the possibility of our having a child together.
Speaker napkin)says : Thank you.
Speaker Leonardsays : Wait a minute– a child? You never see this girl. You just e-mail and text and Twitter. Now you’re considering having a baby?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Amy pointed out that between the two of us, our genetic material has the potential of producing the first in a line of intellectually superior, benign overlords to guide humanity to a brighter tomorrow.
Speaker Howardsays : I’m guessing that future historians will condemn us for not taking this opportunity to kill Sheldon.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, I have a question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : You don’t even like people touching you. How are you going to have sex?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why on Earth would we have sex?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, honey, did your mom not have the talk with you? You know, when your private parts started growing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m quite aware of the way humans usually reproduce, which is messy, unsanitary, and based on living next to you for three years, involves loud and unnecessary appeals to a deity.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, God.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, exactly. Consequently, if Amy and I choose to bring new life into this world, it will be accomplished clinically, with fertility experts in a lab with petri dishes. Which reminds me, you have broad hips and a certain corn-fed vigour. Is your womb available for rental?
Speaker Leonardsays : Still digging the Shamy?
Speaker Pennysays : Look, Sheldon, before you race off to the fertility clinic, you might want to think about, uh gee, I don’t know, maybe actually spending some time with her.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You mean dating?
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I can’t date Amy.
Speaker Pennysays : Why not?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, look, don’t think of it as dating a girlfriend. Think of it as, uh, getting to know the future mother of your child.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh. I hadn’t considered that. I suppose she will have to have access to our progeny. And you don’t think I can achieve the required intimacy via text messaging?
Speaker Pennysays : Probably not.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Huh. It would appear as if the phone companies have been lying to me.

Coversation [January 12, 2012, Session #724]:
Speaker Pennysays : Ooh, look who’s out on a date. Pasadena’s favourite power couple, Shamy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And that is the answer to the question, what is wrong with eating at The Cheesecake Factory?
Speaker Pennysays : So, are we celebrating anything special tonight?
Speaker Amysays : Oh, yes. Our relationship agreement specifies that the second Thursday of every month, or the third Thursday in a month with five Thursdays, is date night.
Speaker Pennysays : That is so hot.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, without objection, the minutes of the previous date are considered read and agreed to. Any new business?
Speaker Amysays : How was your day?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Superb. This morning I made a palindrome with my Alpha-Bits. Nice hat, Bob Tahecin.
Speaker Amysays : Sounds like you hit the ground running. I have a bit of good news myself. My most recent paper on how a cooperative long-term potentiation can map memory sequences in dendritic branches made the cover of Neuron.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Ooh! Speaking of good news, somebody just hit 100 Twitter followers.
Speaker Amysays : That’s nice. Anyway, I’ve been dreaming of this day for a long time.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, me, too. Triple digits, I’m not gonna lie, feels pretty good.
Speaker Amysays : Sheldon, I’m the sole author on a paper being published in a distinguished journal that may change the course of my field.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Mm-hmm. Mm-hmm. Ooh, 101! Air’s getting a bit thin up here.
Speaker Pennysays : So, are we ready to order?
Speaker Amysays : Give me a minute. I’m gonna go wash up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, that’s odd. We both washed up when we came in. It’s probably a euphemism for urination.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, what is wrong with you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Not much. Although, I can be faulted for being overly fond of koala bears. I don’t know what it is, when they smart munching on eucalyptus, I just melt inside.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, Amy just told you some exciting news, and you acted like it was no big deal.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, I see why you’re confused. No, her news sounded important, but what you’re forgetting is, it was an achievement in the field of biology. That’s all about yucky, squishy things.
Speaker Pennysays : Honey, she’s upset. You’re her boyfriend. You have to at least try to be excited by the things she’s excited by.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What if they simply don’t excite me?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, just smile and think about koalas.
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’d see right through that. We go to the zoo all the time. She knows my koala face. And for future reference, it’s this.

Coversation [October 1, 2010, Session #481]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Here’s my chicken curry. Howard, your shrimp biryani.
Speaker Howardsays : Thank you, sir.
Speaker Leonardsays : Palak paneer, that’s Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks.
Speaker Leonardsays : And for Rajesh Koothrappali, from whose homeland these tasty dishes originate, one large order of chicken McNuggets.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, what’s my share?
Speaker Leonardsays : Uh, 12 bucks.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, can I get you after Friday when I get paid?
Speaker Leonardsays : Sure.
Speaker Pennysays : What am I up to now?
Speaker Leonardsays : Well, okay, with the Indian food, the pizza, the Thai food, the tank of gas, the frozen yogurt and your rent, uh, a little over fourteen hundred dollars.
Speaker laugh)says : What now?
Speaker Howardsays : He’s just expressing his admiration that you don’t even have to put out to get free stuff.
Speaker Pennysays : It’s not free, I’m gonna pay him back.  Shut up!
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, are you gonna join us?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Coming! . Greetings, friends.
Speaker Leonardsays : Greetings, whatever the hell you are.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : I am a mobile virtual presence device. Recent events have demonstrated to me that my body is too fragile to endure the vicissitudes of the world. Until such time as I am able to transfer my consciousness, I shall remain in a secure location and interact with the world in this manner.
Speaker him)says : Really? That’s your question? When did he put a ramp in?
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : You’re in my spot. This may seem a little odd at first, but over time you’ll grow accustomed to dealing with me in this configuration.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, to be honest, I don’t see much difference.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : Thank you. That’s what I was going for. Now, Leonard, tomorrow, when we go to work, you’ll need to allow some extra time to get me down the stairs. For your convenience, I disassemble into four pieces.
Speaker Leonardsays : This is ridiculous. I’m coming to talk to you.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : You don’t know where I am. My physical body is safely ensconced in a secure, undisclosed location.
Speaker Leonardsays : You’re in your bedroom.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : No, I’m not.
Speaker Leonardsays : I can hear your voice coming from your bedroom.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : No, you can’t. Wait. Come back. Halt. Authorized personnel only!
Speaker Pennysays : So, either one of you weirdos want to buy my underwear? Only fourteen hundred bucks.

raw_source_record.dialog_embedder_dialog:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [October 15, 2009, Session #330]:
Speaker Sheldonsays : Raj.  Raj.  Raj.
Speaker Rajsays : I’m busy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Doing what?  All right, you’ve made your point.
Speaker Rajsays : What do you want, Sheldon?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I looked over the board and it turns out you were right.
Speaker Rajsays : So you were wrong.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I didn’t say that.
Speaker Rajsays : That’s the only logical inference.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Nevertheless, I didn’t say it. Anyway, I would like you to come back and work for me.
Speaker Rajsays : For you or with you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : In this context for me could mean with me.
Speaker Rajsays : All right, but I have some conditions.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I reject them all.
Speaker Rajsays : I’ll take the job. See you Monday.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Wait, you have to drive me home.
Speaker Rajsays : How did you get here?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I walked.
Speaker Rajsays : So walk home.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I can’t. There’s a big dog outside. On the way home, we can start thinking about methods of optimizing the detector for 500 GEV particles.
Speaker Rajsays : All right.

Coversation [October 30, 2011, Session #691]:
Speaker box)says : Oh, dear.  Oh, dear.  Oh, dear, oh, dear. Oh, dear, oh, dear. Oh, dear. Oh, dear. Purell, Purell, Purell, Purell.
Speaker (entering)says : Good morning, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : It is, isn’t it? Oh, bother. Isn’t that just always the way? You go to staple something, and you’re out of staples. Gosh, I wish I’d known that earlier today when I was at Staples.
Speaker Rajsays : You have a thing of paper clips right there.
Speaker Sheldonsays : well, no, no, I need something more permanent to join these papers. Say, don’t you keep staples in your top desk drawer?
Speaker Rajsays : I don’t know. Maybe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Be a lamb and check.
Speaker Rajsays : All right.  Who do we have here?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a snake. A terrifying snake.
Speaker Rajsays : Oh, did some bad man put us in a drawer?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Stop talking like that. You’ve been rendered speechless by fear.
Speaker Rajsays : Let’s go to the biology lab and find you some nice yummy mice.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I tried to scare an Indian with a snake. Come on, Cooper. You’re better than this.

Coversation [January 11, 2010, Session #378]:
Speaker Rajsays : Thanks for coming with me.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Thanks for giving me your limited edition Green Lantern lantern.
Speaker Rajsays : Did you really have to bring it in with you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : What if evil strikes and my power ring runs low?
Speaker Rajsays : Come on, let’s get a drink.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I don’t drink.
Speaker Rajsays : Yeah, well I do. And when my wingman is carrying a Green Lantern lantern, I drink a lot. I’ll have a screwdriver, please. Don’t be chintzy with the screw.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I would like a root beer float.
Speaker Rajsays : Sheldon, they don’t have ice cream.
Speaker Sheldonsays : They don’t? Well, apparently, these people and I differ greatly on the definition of party.
Speaker Rajsays : He’ll have a Shirley Temple.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And don’t be chintzy with the Shirley.
Speaker Rajsays : Okay, let’s check out the females.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right. There’s a female.
Speaker Rajsays : That’s Professor Wilkinson’s wife, she’s like 80 years old.
Speaker Sheldonsays : But she’s female. Isn’t that the game?
Speaker Rajsays : No. I’m looking for a hookup.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, yes. So, the point of this exercise is for you to find someone to copulate with?
Speaker Rajsays : Not so loud, but ideally, yes. Thanks.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Thank you. And what is my function as wingman?
Speaker Rajsays : You help me run my game.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay. What is your game?
Speaker Sheldonsays : When I lie through my teeth to a woman, you nod and agree.
Speaker Girlsays : Hey, that’s pretty cool. What is it?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a limited edition Green Lantern lantern. My friend is looking for someone to copulate with.
Speaker Girlsays : You’re very funny. I’m Abby.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m Sheldon. How do you do?
Speaker Rajsays : How do you do? Rajesh Ramayan Koothrappali. Call me Raj.
Speaker Abbysays : Hi, Raj. Where are you from?
Speaker Rajsays : The mysterious subcontinent of India.
Speaker Abbysays : Ooh, India.
Speaker Rajsays : You know India?
Speaker Abbysays : I saw Slumdog Millionaire.
Speaker Rajsays : Well, I’m a slumdog astrophysicist.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I thought your father was a gynaecologist. I’m sorry.
Speaker Abbysays : Martha, come here. Meet Raj and Sheldon. This is my friend, Martha.
Speaker Marthasays : Hi.
Speaker Rajsays : Hello.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hello.
Speaker Marthasays : Is that the limited edition Green Lantern lantern?
Speaker Sheldonsays : In brightest day, in darkest night, no evil shall escape my sight.
Speaker Marthasays : Oh, that is so awesome.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Thank you. Have you chosen one to copulate with?

Coversation [October 9, 2011, Session #670]:
Speaker Pennysays : Mustache is looking good there, Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Don’t thank me. Thank the dice. They told me what percentage of my face to shave.
Speaker Howardsays : Why are you still doing this?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Because it’s working. In the past few weeks, unburdened by trivial decisions, I’ve co-authored two papers in notable peer-reviewed journals, and I’m close to figuring out why the Large Hadron Collider has yet to isolate the Higgs boson particle.
Speaker Leonardsays : You left out, got chafed testicles because you no longer wear underpants.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The dice giveth and the dice taketh away.
Speaker Pennysays : Is Raj out with Emily again?
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, every night for the last month.
Speaker Pennysays : Wow, can’t believe he has a girlfriend.
Speaker dice)says : Me neither.
Speaker Howardsays : Here’s some other fun news on the Raj/Emily front. He gave her a pair of diamond earrings and leased her a car.
Speaker Pennysays : You’re kidding.
Speaker Leonardsays : You think she’s taking advantage of him?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, of course not. She wouldn’t do something like that. She’s deaf.
Speaker Leonardsays : Deaf women can’t be gold diggers?
Speaker Pennysays : Handicapped people are nice, Leonard. Everyone knows that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, I actually have information about Raj that would be helpful with this discussion.
Speaker Leonardsays : Could you tell us?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Let’s see.  Snake eyes. Sorry, bud.
Speaker Pennysays : Wait, hang on. Doubles. Roll again.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Okay, get this. It doesn’t matter if he’s showering her with gifts, because the Koothrappalis are vastly wealthy.
Speaker Pennysays : What do you mean, vastly wealthy?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, wealthy means a lot of money, and vastly means even more. I’m not sure what’s tripping you up.
Speaker Leonardsays : Look, I know they have money. I don’t think it’s that much.
Speaker Sheldonsays : No, you’re wrong. See, as you know, a few years ago, I achieved one of my lesser dreams and became a notary public. Well, from time to time, I notarize banking documents for Raj. The Koothrappalis aren’t just rich, they’re Richie Rich rich.
Speaker Pennysays : Well, so how much is that?
Speaker Sheldonsays : About halfway between Bruce Wayne and Scrooge McDuck.
Speaker Howardsays : What the hell? The last time we went to the zoo, that son of a bitch made me buy him a churro.
Speaker Leonardsays : Listen, guys, I’m sorry, I don’t mean to be rude, but I need to go call Raj’s sister, who I love so much. So vastly much.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, so he’s got money, and it’s a few gifts and a car.
Speaker Howardsays : And she got him to pay off all her credit cards.
Speaker Pennysays : What? He paid off her credit cards? Damn it, I could’ve dated Raj for a couple months. But I, I wouldn’t have, because I’m not that kind of girl. We should really talk to Raj.
Speaker Howardsays : He’s not going to listen, he’s in love.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Can’t figure out what to do? I remember those days. Now, if you’ll excuse me, I have to…  stay right here.

Coversation [August 25, 2008, Session #158]:
Speaker Leonardsays : And when we go in there, let’s show Raj that we’re happy for him.
Speaker Sheldonsays : But I’m not.
Speaker Howardsays : Well then fake it. Look at me, I could be grinding on the fact that without my stabilizing telescope mount he never would have found that stupid little clump of cosmic schmutz, but I’m bigger than that.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine, what do you want me to do?
Speaker Leonardsays : Smile.
Speaker Howardsays : Oh crap, that’s terrifying.
Speaker Leonardsays : We’re here to see Koothrappali, not kill Batman.
Speaker Howardsays : Try less teeth.
Speaker Leonardsays : Close enough, come on.  Hi Raj.
Speaker Rajsays : Hey guys, what’s up?
Speaker Howardsays : We just wanted to invite you out to dinner tonight.
Speaker Leonardsays : Celebrate your thirty under thirty thing. Right Sheldon?
Speaker Rajsays : It’s very nice of you, I would like that.
Speaker (entering)says : Hello boys.
Speaker Rajsays : Dr Gablehouser.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Dr Koothrappali.
Speaker Leonardsays : Dr Gablehauser.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Dr Hoffstadter.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Dr Gablehauser.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Dr Cooper.
Speaker Howardsays : Dr Gablehauser.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Mister Wolowitz. Boys, I’ve got a question for you. Who in this room discovered a star?
Speaker Rajsays : Actually, 2008 NQ sub 17 is a planetary body.
Speaker Gablehausersays : I’m not talking about you, I’m talking about me. You, my exotic young friend are my star.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, you didn’t discover him, you merely noticed he was here, much like he did with 2008 NQ sub 17.
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon!
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, sorry.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Well, we’ve got to get you into a better office, something more suited to your status.
Speaker Rajsays : Really, you don’t have to go to any trouble.
Speaker Gablehausersays : How about if I put you in Von Gerlick’s old office?
Speaker Rajsays : I’d rather have Fishbine’s, it’s bigger.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Done.
Speaker Howardsays : Wait a minute, I called dibs on Fishbine’s office the day he started showing up at work in his bathrobe.
Speaker Sheldonsays : He gets a new office, I can’t even get paper towels in the men’s room?
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Damn, this is hard.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Let me ask you something, what do you think the business of this place is?
Speaker other)says : Science?
Speaker Gablehausersays : Money.
Speaker Howardsays : Told you.
Speaker Gablehausersays : And this boy’s picture in People magazine is going to raise us a pile of money taller than… well, taller than you
Speaker Howardsays : I have a master’s degree.
Speaker Gablehausersays : Who doesn’t? Dr Koothrappali, have you ever had lunch in the President’s dining room?
Speaker Rajsays : I didn’t even know there was a President’s dining room.
Speaker Gablehausersays : It’s the same food as the Cafeteria, only fresh. Come on little buddy.
Speaker Rajsays : Okay, big buddy. See you tonight guys.
Speaker Leonardsays : You can stop smiling now.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Aaaah!

supporting_material.dialog_context:
[{"role": "user", "content": "Context:
Coversation [January 20, 2009, Session #227]:
Speaker Pennysays : Hello Sheldon.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Hi.
Speaker Pennysays : What is this?
Speaker Sheldonsays : It’s a questionnaire I devised. I’m having some difficulty bonding with a colleague at work, so I’m doing a little research to better understand why my current friends like me.
Speaker Pennysays : Yes. Well, that is a good question. But is this really the best way to figure it out?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, I agree, the social sciences are largely hokum. But, short of putting electrodes in your brain and monitoring your response to my companionship, this is the best I can do.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, question 1. Rank the following aspects of Sheldon Cooper in order of appeal. Intelligence. Ruthless attention to hygiene. Playfulness. Java applet writing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know, I may have started off with a fairly obvious one. You did an aspect of my most appealing trait, playfulness. Why don’t you just go ahead and write that number 1. I’m afraid you’re on your own for the rest. It should take you no more than 3 hours.
Speaker Pennysays : Wait! How many questions are on this thing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Only 211. Don’t worry, in deference to you, I’ve kept them on a high school graduate reading level.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks pal.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You got it, buddy.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon honey, did you ever consider making friends by being, I don’t know, pleasant?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well that’s certainly a thought-provoking hypothesis. May I suggest it as the topic for your essay.

Coversation [November 17, 2011, Session #709]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Hey, can I talk to you about something? It’s a little awkward.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I know what this is about. Given the professional standstill you’re at, you’re wondering if this is the appropriate time to abandon your research and focus on teaching. Yes. And if I may suggest, consider changing disciplines. Yeah, to the humanities, perhaps history. One of the advantages of teaching history is that you don’t have to create things, you know, you just have to remember stuff that happened and then parrot it back. You could have fun with that.
Speaker Leonardsays : Yeah, that’s not it. Stuart’s kind of interested in Amy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, of course he is. She’s very interesting. Did you know, when she was 14, she severed the webbing between her own toes?
Speaker Leonardsays : No. He wanted me to find out if you’d have a problem with him asking her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m not sure how to respond, Leonard. I don’t own Amy. Can’t own a person. At least not since? 1863. President Lincoln freed the? Slaves! Come on Leonard, if you’re going to teach history, these are the kind of facts you’ll have to know.
Speaker Leonardsays : You know what? Never mind. I’m going to tell him it’s okay to ask her out.
Speaker Sheldonsays : The question is moot. There is no way that Stuart, an impoverished peddler of picture books, would be at all appealing to Amy Farrah Fowler, a noted neurobiologist capable of performing surgery on her own feet with nothing but nitrous oxide from cans of whipped cream as anesthesia.
Speaker Leonardsays : All right, but just for the record, I checked in with you to see how you’d feel about it.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Fine. The record shall so reflect. Now, getting back to the problem at hand, what to do with a washed-up experimental physicist.
Speaker Leonardsays : I am not washed-up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, Leonard. Lots of people love you and want to help you, but they can’t until you admit the problem.

Coversation [September 23, 2010, Session #471]:
Speaker Pennysays : Does NASA know you’re using that thing as a napkin holder?
Speaker Howardsays : You kidding? They still think it’s in a secure locker at JPL.
Speaker Pennysays : You stole it?
Speaker Howardsays : Borrowed. The trick is to carry it out to your car like you own it.
Speaker alert)says : Excuse me. Oh. Amy’s at the dry cleaners, and she’s made a very amusing pun. “I don’t care for perchloroethylene, and I don’t like glycol ether.” Get it? She doesn’t like glycol ether. Sounds like either.  L-O-L.
Speaker Pennysays : Who’s Amy?
Speaker Leonardsays : His girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon has a girlfriend?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : How long has this been going on?
Speaker Leonardsays : Four months.
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Are you telling me, for the past four months I have been asking you what’s new and you never thought to go with Sheldon has a girlfriend?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Ah, du-du-du-du-du. How did they meet?
Speaker Howardsays : Raj and I entered Sheldon’s information on a dating site, and it spit out Amy Farrah Fowler.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, my God! Sheldon and Amy.
Speaker Howardsays : Or, as we call them, Shamy.
Speaker (squeals)says : Shamy. I am so digging the Shamy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, everyone pay attention. Yes, I have a friend named Amy Farrah Fowler. Yes, she is female. Yes, we communicate on a daily basis, but no, she is not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, well, what do you communicate about?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, my work in physics, her work in neurobiology, and most recently, the possibility of our having a child together.
Speaker napkin)says : Thank you.
Speaker Leonardsays : Wait a minute– a child? You never see this girl. You just e-mail and text and Twitter. Now you’re considering having a baby?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Amy pointed out that between the two of us, our genetic material has the potential of producing the first in a line of intellectually superior, benign overlords to guide humanity to a brighter tomorrow.
Speaker Howardsays : I’m guessing that future historians will condemn us for not taking this opportunity to kill Sheldon.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, I have a question.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : You don’t even like people touching you. How are you going to have sex?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Why on Earth would we have sex?
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, honey, did your mom not have the talk with you? You know, when your private parts started growing?
Speaker Sheldonsays : I’m quite aware of the way humans usually reproduce, which is messy, unsanitary, and based on living next to you for three years, involves loud and unnecessary appeals to a deity.
Speaker Pennysays : Oh, God.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yes, exactly. Consequently, if Amy and I choose to bring new life into this world, it will be accomplished clinically, with fertility experts in a lab with petri dishes. Which reminds me, you have broad hips and a certain corn-fed vigour. Is your womb available for rental?
Speaker Leonardsays : Still digging the Shamy?
Speaker Pennysays : Look, Sheldon, before you race off to the fertility clinic, you might want to think about, uh gee, I don’t know, maybe actually spending some time with her.
Speaker Sheldonsays : You mean dating?
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah.
Speaker Sheldonsays : I can’t date Amy.
Speaker Pennysays : Why not?
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’s not my girlfriend.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, look, don’t think of it as dating a girlfriend. Think of it as, uh, getting to know the future mother of your child.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh. I hadn’t considered that. I suppose she will have to have access to our progeny. And you don’t think I can achieve the required intimacy via text messaging?
Speaker Pennysays : Probably not.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Huh. It would appear as if the phone companies have been lying to me.

Coversation [January 12, 2012, Session #724]:
Speaker Pennysays : Ooh, look who’s out on a date. Pasadena’s favourite power couple, Shamy.
Speaker Sheldonsays : And that is the answer to the question, what is wrong with eating at The Cheesecake Factory?
Speaker Pennysays : So, are we celebrating anything special tonight?
Speaker Amysays : Oh, yes. Our relationship agreement specifies that the second Thursday of every month, or the third Thursday in a month with five Thursdays, is date night.
Speaker Pennysays : That is so hot.
Speaker Sheldonsays : All right, without objection, the minutes of the previous date are considered read and agreed to. Any new business?
Speaker Amysays : How was your day?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Superb. This morning I made a palindrome with my Alpha-Bits. Nice hat, Bob Tahecin.
Speaker Amysays : Sounds like you hit the ground running. I have a bit of good news myself. My most recent paper on how a cooperative long-term potentiation can map memory sequences in dendritic branches made the cover of Neuron.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Ooh! Speaking of good news, somebody just hit 100 Twitter followers.
Speaker Amysays : That’s nice. Anyway, I’ve been dreaming of this day for a long time.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Yeah, me, too. Triple digits, I’m not gonna lie, feels pretty good.
Speaker Amysays : Sheldon, I’m the sole author on a paper being published in a distinguished journal that may change the course of my field.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Mm-hmm. Mm-hmm. Ooh, 101! Air’s getting a bit thin up here.
Speaker Pennysays : So, are we ready to order?
Speaker Amysays : Give me a minute. I’m gonna go wash up.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Well, that’s odd. We both washed up when we came in. It’s probably a euphemism for urination.
Speaker Pennysays : Sheldon, what is wrong with you?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Not much. Although, I can be faulted for being overly fond of koala bears. I don’t know what it is, when they smart munching on eucalyptus, I just melt inside.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, Amy just told you some exciting news, and you acted like it was no big deal.
Speaker Sheldonsays : Oh, I see why you’re confused. No, her news sounded important, but what you’re forgetting is, it was an achievement in the field of biology. That’s all about yucky, squishy things.
Speaker Pennysays : Honey, she’s upset. You’re her boyfriend. You have to at least try to be excited by the things she’s excited by.
Speaker Sheldonsays : What if they simply don’t excite me?
Speaker Pennysays : Well, just smile and think about koalas.
Speaker Sheldonsays : She’d see right through that. We go to the zoo all the time. She knows my koala face. And for future reference, it’s this.

Coversation [October 1, 2010, Session #481]:
Speaker Leonardsays : Here’s my chicken curry. Howard, your shrimp biryani.
Speaker Howardsays : Thank you, sir.
Speaker Leonardsays : Palak paneer, that’s Penny.
Speaker Pennysays : Thanks.
Speaker Leonardsays : And for Rajesh Koothrappali, from whose homeland these tasty dishes originate, one large order of chicken McNuggets.
Speaker Pennysays : Hey, what’s my share?
Speaker Leonardsays : Uh, 12 bucks.
Speaker Pennysays : Okay, can I get you after Friday when I get paid?
Speaker Leonardsays : Sure.
Speaker Pennysays : What am I up to now?
Speaker Leonardsays : Well, okay, with the Indian food, the pizza, the Thai food, the tank of gas, the frozen yogurt and your rent, uh, a little over fourteen hundred dollars.
Speaker laugh)says : What now?
Speaker Howardsays : He’s just expressing his admiration that you don’t even have to put out to get free stuff.
Speaker Pennysays : It’s not free, I’m gonna pay him back.  Shut up!
Speaker Leonardsays : Sheldon, are you gonna join us?
Speaker Sheldonsays : Coming! . Greetings, friends.
Speaker Leonardsays : Greetings, whatever the hell you are.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : I am a mobile virtual presence device. Recent events have demonstrated to me that my body is too fragile to endure the vicissitudes of the world. Until such time as I am able to transfer my consciousness, I shall remain in a secure location and interact with the world in this manner.
Speaker him)says : Really? That’s your question? When did he put a ramp in?
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : You’re in my spot. This may seem a little odd at first, but over time you’ll grow accustomed to dealing with me in this configuration.
Speaker Pennysays : Yeah, to be honest, I don’t see much difference.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : Thank you. That’s what I was going for. Now, Leonard, tomorrow, when we go to work, you’ll need to allow some extra time to get me down the stairs. For your convenience, I disassemble into four pieces.
Speaker Leonardsays : This is ridiculous. I’m coming to talk to you.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : You don’t know where I am. My physical body is safely ensconced in a secure, undisclosed location.
Speaker Leonardsays : You’re in your bedroom.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : No, I’m not.
Speaker Leonardsays : I can hear your voice coming from your bedroom.
Speaker Sheldon-botsays : No, you can’t. Wait. Come back. Halt. Authorized personnel only!
Speaker Pennysays : So, either one of you weirdos want to buy my underwear? Only fourteen hundred bucks.
